location.office_time_required: Monday-Thursday every week in-office in San Francisco

level_signals: Prior ownership of pricing, monetization, or P&L-adjacent responsibilities
Base salary range $207K–$280K, consistent with senior/staff IC at a late-stage fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6–10+ years of Product Management experience on consumer-facing, revenue-generating products
Prior ownership of pricing, monetization, or P&L-adjacent responsibilities
Experience operating in regulated environments (FinTech, payments, lending, banking, or adjacent)
A track record of leading complex, cross-functional initiatives with executive-level stakeholders
Strong data fluency, including experimentation, metrics definition, and financial modeling
Experience owning full-stack product surfaces (backend systems + user-facing experiences)
Comfort operating under pressure with high external and internal visibility

scope.surface: MyPay — Chime's Earned Wage Access (EWA) product; full-stack product including pricing, backend systems, and user-facing experiences
scope.business_model: B2C fintech; EWA/earned wage access product, one of largest revenue-driving businesses at Chime
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end product strategy and execution for MyPay, Chime's EWA product
Frame ambiguous, high-stakes problems and translate them into clear product bets, hypotheses, and roadmaps
Balance short-term iteration with long-term vision, ensuring the product compounds member trust over time
Act as the unofficial GM of the business, owning pricing, growth, profitability, and long-term roadmap
Partner closely with Finance, Legal, Risk, Compliance, Data, Marketing, Operations, and Engineering
Anticipate regulatory change and translate ambiguity into clear product direction and execution plans
Present strategy, results, and tradeoffs to executive leadership
Drive cross-functional alignment across design, engineering, analytics, operations, and marketing

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated and competitive ($207K–$280K base) with equity and bonus
Clear product ownership area — MyPay EWA, described as one of the largest businesses at Chime
High executive visibility signals meaningful scope and impact
Explicit mention of regulatory complexity signals substantive domain challenges
Bonus, equity, and robust benefits package described in detail
red_flags: Role is in-office Monday–Thursday in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates
JD body states Monday–Thursday in-office but benefits section describes 'four days a week in the office and Fridays from home' — minor wording inconsistency, effectively the same policy
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international applicants
High external/regulatory scrutiny and executive visibility may indicate a high-pressure environment

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + responsibilities; not explicitly labeled in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and 'revenue-driving product' language)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Actual compensation may vary based on location')
ambiguities: JD body says 'in-office in San Francisco Monday-Thursday every week' while the benefits section describes the general policy as 'four days a week in the office and Fridays from home' — treated as consistent onsite policy for this role
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere; work_auth set to 'open' and visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear'
years_required_max is ambiguous — JD states '6–10+ years' which could be read as a range or a floor; treated as minimum 6 with no hard cap
